reasoning: The sinks are confirmed real: wp_head() (line 174) echoes get_option('jamify_hfs_insert_header') verbatim; wp_body_open() (line 190) echoes get_option('jamify_hfs_insert_body') verbatim; wp_footer() (lines 200-205) runs get_option('jamify_hfs_insert_footer') through convert_smilies() and do_shortcode() then echoes it verbatim; per-post meta is echoed at line 179 as $shfs_post_meta['synth_header_script']. All phpcs:ignore comments confirm intentional unescaped output. Two write paths exist: (1) Global options via register_setting() with only 'trim' sanitization, gated behind manage_options (admin), not unfiltered_html as the finding claims; (2) Per-post meta via post_meta_save(), which does gate on current_user_can('unfiltered_html') at line 320. The finding's claim that line 320 gates the global options write path is incorrect -- global options only require manage_options. The optional privilege escalation is confirmed real: update_role_author() (line ~248) and update_role_contributor() (line 263) explicitly call $role->add_cap('unfiltered_html') when options 'jamify_hfs_allow_author'/'jamify_hfs_allow_contributor' are set to 'yes'. When an admin enables either option, Authors or Contributors can write per-post meta that is echoed verbatim in wp_head for that post. On multisite, unfiltered_html is super-admin only regardless of these settings, limiting the per-post meta path. The global options path is strictly admin-only on both single-site and multisite. The do_shortcode() execution in wp_footer is a real additional concern as it executes registered shortcodes stored within the footer script value. Severity medium is appropriate: the primary admin-only global options path is by-design for WordPress (admins are trusted); the escalation path to Author/Contributor via unfiltered_html grants is the genuinely concerning vector and is the real security risk in this plugin.
